TextENGNew: ......I crouched there for some time, and... when full awareness returned, the room had completely changed.
The numb ringing in my skull receded, and... slowly... I could feel circulation returning throughout my body.
How long... had I been huddled here...?
Minutes?
Or tens of minutes...?
changeReason: Japanese '何十分' means 'tens of minutes'; original mistranslates as 'hours' while new correctly says 'tens of minutes.'

TextENGNew: ......The hands on the clock overhead had barely moved at all -- as though they'd stopped entirely during the time my eyes had been closed.
...Had they, really?
The air filling the room now was no longer the madness-soaked thing from before... only a gray silence remained.
Rena, who should have been pinning my arms from behind, was gone. Mion, who had been pressing in with the syringe, was gone too.
......Could it be... all of it... was some kind of... hallucination?!
There was no presence in the room other than my own...
It was the most abnormal experience I'd ever had.
...........I was certain...... Rena and Mion had been........
For an instant I doubted my own sanity, but at the same time, I felt a certain kind of relief.
Ha ha ha, ......so after all...... that terrifying ordeal... was just a hallucination.
Neither Rena nor Mion... would ever do... something so terrible...
The corners of my eyes... burned.
I could feel emotion welling up inside me...
Why...?
That wasn't the question that made my tears rise.
Why...?
......It was... sorrow.
......Why did I feel such sadness...?
I didn't understand.@k ......I didn't understand......
Regardless of gender or age... they'd been kind to me without reservation.
...Mion.
......That Mion lay sprawled by the window in an unnatural pose.
From her head down to her chest, she was covered in a sticky dark red.
The bright crimson spattered across the wall... had undoubtedly come from Mion.
She always wore that bright smile, and she'd been kind to me from my very first day after transferring.
...Rena.
...That Rena lay crumpled at my feet, forming the same kind of pool of blood as Mion.
I couldn't comprehend what had happened.
......Had someone come to save me?
Had they struck these two down?! ...With... this metal bat...?
I finally noticed the weight in my right arm.
When had I picked it up?
......It was Satoshi's metal bat.
It was slathered in dark, sticky blood... there was no doubting it was the weapon that had beaten the two of them down.
And I was the one holding that unmistakable murder weapon.
There was no one else in the house.
Objectively speaking, the only possible conclusion was that I had done this.
...That's right.@k Maebara Keiichi.
Of course it was you.
I... told myself gently... as though consoling a child.
Hey, me.
...You don't have to force yourself to remember, and there's no need for regret.
......It was kill or be killed.
changeReason: Japanese 'やらなきゃやられてた' literally means 'if I didn't do it, it would be done to me'; original's 'Eat or be eaten' and new's 'kill or be killed' differ -- new is more literally violent.

TextENGNew: You understand that, don't you...?
``But...... the blood... so much blood... there's so much......!``
Neither Rena nor Mion had so much as twitched.
This wasn't something as mild as a cracked forehead and a trickle of blood.
...Bright red spray was scattered across the entire room, testifying that the blows had not stopped at two or three.
Deep down I was calm, but the surface of my mind was all panic and turmoil.
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi.
Where was your usual composure?!
Come on, now.@k ...Do what you always do -- tilt your head back and take a long, deep breath.@k ...Come on.
...Once.@w800. ...Twice.@w800. ...A deep, deep breath.
Calm down.
Calm down...
I repeated it over and over in my head, steadying myself...
At last, color returned to the world, and the air regained its scent.
And along with it... I began to recall what had happened during that blank interval...
Rena and Mion had attacked me.
They'd tried to inject me with something that would 'cause the same symptoms as Tomitake-san.'
And just before they could... I'd fought back.
I'd twisted my whole body and thrown off Rena, who had me in a full nelson.
Still spinning, I'd driven my heel as hard as I could into Mion's abdomen.@k ...It was soft.
When Rena lunged at me again, I body-slammed her into the wall with everything I had.
Both of them were off balance, and I didn't let that split-second opening go to waste.
Satoshi's bat, left carelessly beside my desk......!!!

changeReason: Japanese 'ぷつん' is a snapping/disconnecting sound; original's 'Bweep' (electronic beep) conveys a different sound than new's 'snap' which matches the Japanese.

TextENGNew: And there... everything went black.
It was as if the videotape of my consciousness simply hadn't recorded what came after...
No... that wasn't right.
......It wasn't that nothing had been recorded.
It had been recorded perfectly.
It was just that... the other me inside... had told me not to watch, and switched off the television.
Just because the screen went dark and I couldn't see it... didn't mean the tape inside me had stopped recording.
The television was just switched off... the playback was still running.
The videotape inside me... clicking along... still playing.
On the far side of that pitch-black screen... that horrifying footage was still running...
Even compared to that, though...
...The carnage before my eyes... was still impossible to accept easily.
Blood was splattered across every wall in the room, and two bodies lay in unnatural poses.
...Motionless.
...I couldn't even tell if they were breathing......
Whatever the circumstances had been...... I had beaten them. Girls. My friends. ......I had beaten them.
...I may have even killed them.
But...... if I hadn't done this, I would have been the one finished off.
Weighed on that scale... it seemed almost absurd that I was agonizing over it...
Excessive as it may have been... this was legitimate self-defense.
All the evidence was right here.
The two of them on the floor, and Mion's syringe.
The unknown substance in Mion's syringe would surely unravel the mystery of Tomitake-san's case.
And from the fact that these two were involved, they could work their way through the chain and arrest every last conspirator.
I might still be charged with a crime.@k ......But so be it.
At the very least, this would now be a proper police matter.
It wouldn't be swept under the rug like the incidents in Rena's past.
As long as the police were involved...... everything would finally be settled.
They would reopen their investigation into the entire chain of past incidents.
......Ooishi-san would surely... uncover everything.
In other words...... my wish.
...I don't want to die.
I want to know the full truth...... would be fulfilled, at least in its most basic form.
It would be realized soon enough.
The doctor Rena had called would be arriving any moment.
I would confess everything.
But before that, I should contact Ooishi-san as well......
At that moment, I remembered.
Besides the doctor, another figure -- referred to as 'the director' -- had also been summoned.
From Rena and Mion's conversation, it was easy to imagine this was someone deeply involved in the incidents.
......Even the luxury of feeling heartsick over this gruesome scene was rapidly draining away.
It was... not over yet.
This place... was no longer safe.
Stay cool, Maebara Keiichi.
...It's not over yet.
Until I could get this incident to the police...... I had to stay alive...!!
...Just then, I thought I heard voices coming from outside.
Voices meant conversation, and conversation meant more than one person.
I shifted the curtain ever so slightly and peered out.
......It was a bizarre sight.
Four or five adult men were clustered in front of the gate.
Their aura strongly resembled the two men who had attacked me at the dam site today.
...Those two might even be among them.
One of them was wearing a white coat.
......But he looked nothing like a doctor.@k My instinct told me it was a disguise.
He was probably the one designated to ring the doorbell and get me to open the front door.
Get me to open the door under the pretense of being a doctor...... then the rest of them would rush in all at once...
Then I saw the vehicle parked behind the men, and my heart nearly leapt out of my chest.
No doubt about it.
...The same one... that had tried to run me down...?!?!
The man in the white coat entered the gate and approached the front door.
The other men lurked in the shadow of the bushes, watching him...
Pretending I wasn't home would likely be pointless.
...Men like them would simply smash a window and force their way in...!
...I had to escape this house somehow.
Then... find a public phone and contact Ooishi-san.
We'd meet up somewhere.
First, I needed a weapon!@k And shoes!!
But before any of that... there was one thing I absolutely had to do...!!
...I had no intention of dying.
I would survive...... and expose the truth behind this insane 'Curse of Oyashiro-sama'...!!!
But... depending on what happened next...... regardless of my resolve...... I might meet my end.
......And that was precisely why.
...This was more important than escaping right now.
I quickly took down the clock where I'd hidden the note and peeled off the memo taped behind it.
...Damn it...!@k I'd taped it on so thoroughly that it wouldn't come loose...!!
A few tears in the paper didn't matter.
I unfolded the slightly torn note and added a new passage with a marker.
......If I couldn't manage to tell Ooishi-san in person...@k ......this note was my only lifeline.
......Never before had a single page torn from a cheap notebook felt so vital.
There was no time. Only the truths I knew right now.@k ...I had to leave behind whatever information might help uncover the truth!!
'Rena and Mion are among the perpetrators.'
That was an undeniable fact.@k ...I still didn't want to believe it.@k ...But it was the truth!
Anyway... I'd leave every scrap of information that could lead to the culprits.
'There are at least four or five other adults.@k They own a white van.'
...That was all I'd been able to see from the window.@k ...There might be more.
And then... the unknown entity called 'the director.'
...To begin with... 'director' was a word that didn't belong in Hinamizawa.
......If there was any context in the past incidents where the term 'director' appeared...... it was only the construction foreman -- the victim of the very first murder.
The very first victim in the chain of incidents.
......Hadn't the police confirmed that death...?
But Rena and Mion had definitely said they'd called 'the director.'
They'd said 'the director.'
You can't summon a dead person.
...Even the police would never suspect that someone confirmed dead could be involved.
......Was that... some kind of blind spot??
I didn't know.
But... even if I couldn't make sense of it... it might be a crucial clue for Ooishi-san.
...Right.
......They needed to reinvestigate from the very first incident.
If that hadn't been merely a revenge killing, but the beginning of the chain of mysterious deaths that followed......then something had to be hidden there!
'Please reinvestigate the victim from the murder incident four years ago.@k He is alive.'
A person's death should be thoroughly verified through autopsy and investigation.@k ......Logically, I understood that.
...But was that really the case?
Could there have been some cunning trick that deceived even the police...??
I couldn't say for certain.@k ......But... he might still be alive...
There was far too little time to think that through now.
......Right.
There was something even more important I needed to write...!
'Tomitake-san's death was caused by an unknown drug.'
Yes.@k This drug... was the one irreplaceable piece of evidence!
With this alone... everything could surely be brought to light...!!!
I couldn't leave such vital evidence lying on the floor.
'The attached syringe is proof.'
Writing that, I taped the evidence syringe firmly to the back of the clock.
So it absolutely wouldn't fall loose...... securely...... securely!!
Ding-dong...
The doorbell rang.
......They were here!!!
I couldn't write any more.
......And yet!@k There was one last thing...... I couldn't leave unwritten.
'I have no idea why any of this happened.'
...........................Of everything I'd written on this note, this might be the closest thing to the truth.
'If you are reading this, then I am probably already dead.@k ...Whether or not my body is found may be the only difference.'
changeReason: Quote style changes from backticks to single quotes, and 'Though you may or may not find my body' becomes 'Whether or not my body is found may be the only difference' -- the new version more accurately conveys the Japanese nuance about the only variable being whether a body exists.

TextENGNew: ...........................If I was going to cover every possible outcome.
...Would I die by the curse?
Or vanish through spiriting-away......
'To whoever reads this.@k Please uncover the truth.@k That is my sole wish.'
With this...... my last will and testament was complete.
It wasn't certain that I was going to die.
...But... it was my final plea...... just in case.
I folded the note, stuck it to the back of the clock, and returned the clock to its place on the wall.
I couldn't help but pray.
......Ooishi-san.
...If anything happens to me... I'm leaving it all to you...!!
Then I looked down at Rena and Mion, lying still on the floor.
...This was probably our final parting.@k Rena.@k Mion.
``I...... truly thought...... we were all friends......``
And yet...... how did it come to this......?
At my previous school, there had been nothing enjoyable.
...All I did was obsess over test scores, fretting about whether my target school was 'within reach' or 'safely in range'... that was all anyone ever talked about.
...It had been a gray existence.
The people I called friends were really just academic rivals, competitors battling over internal rankings and recommendation letters.
It was everyone here who taught me just how unhealthy that life had been.
This past month was truly, genuinely fun...
Making a big fuss over lunch boxes, making a big fuss over club activities, making a big fuss at the festival......
Something hot was pouring from my eyes in heavy drops.
......Before I knew it... they were tears.
I had no reason to shed tears for them.
...But I couldn't stop them...!!
Even if they had been after my life.
Even if they had tried to kill me.
...The memories from this one month...... I could never forget them...
Or had...... those happy days...... all been a lie...?
Had it all been a trap...... meticulously maintained until today...... to ensnare me?
Had I been the only one who believed we were friends.........?
That couldn't...... be true!!!!
Rena and Mion...... they really were my friends!!
Those happy days...... there wasn't a single cloud of deceit in any of them...!!!
Surely, Rena and the others...... had been coerced by someone into trying to kill me.
changeReason: New translation preserves 'Rena and the others' (レナたち) from the Japanese, which the original omits by using generic 'them.'

TextENGNew: Or... a supernatural entity called Oyashiro-sama had possessed them, hijacking their consciousness!
Regardless...... both Rena and Mion...... were the greatest friends I'd ever had!!
And... when they came after me...... it was not of their own will!!!!!
But... no matter what pressure they might have been under, they weren't the type to betray a friend.
And something as fantastical as being taken over by Oyashiro-sama couldn't possibly happen in reality.
Then... after all...... the real Rena and Mion had... attacked me...?!?!
What... was I even thinking............
It was a ridiculous, stupid line of thought.
...Having beaten Rena and Mion to the ground...
...I was still debating whether they had been the real ones or imposters.
There were no real or fake versions of them.
...Only the outcome was the truth.
Rena and Mion lay at my feet -- that alone was the truth...!!
I was just trying to conveniently twist the fact...... that I had beaten my friends to death......!!
No matter how I twisted it...... the reality that Rena and Mion were dead...... would not change...!!!!
I felt a crack forming in the dam that had been holding back my deranged emotions.
......The composure that was nothing more than bravado receded, and through the gap...... I could feel madness seeping in...
I killed them.
I killed them.
...Rena and Mion...@k I killed them...!!
The doorbell rang again.
Its merciless peal restored my composure one final time.
There wasn't a moment left to spare!@k I had to... run!!!
I didn't want to die, no matter what.@k And then I would uncover everything.
The true identity of whatever had driven me to this point......!!!
Even if I had to drink muddy water and eat grass to survive...!!
I would survive. I would survive. I would absolutely survive!!
It was for that reason I'd killed Rena.
And killed Mion.
I had gone that far just to stay alive.
...So I couldn't die now.
For the sake of Rena and Mion, who had been killed so that I could survive...
I absolutely had to live on!!!!
When I dashed to the front door and snatched up my shoes, the doorbell rang again, as if to hurry me along.
......Behind this single door...... they were right there!!!
Suppressing my footsteps... I headed to the kitchen.@k ...To the back door.
changeReason: Japanese '足音を殺しながら' specifically means 'suppressing/killing my footsteps'; original's 'Keeping quiet' is less specific than new's 'Suppressing my footsteps.'

TextENGNew: Before throwing the back door open, I pressed my ear against it to check for anyone outside......
No presence...?
I put on my shoes... and opened the door, slowly, silently...
``There he is!! The back door!!``
A sharp voice rang out.
...It pierced through me...... and a shudder tore through my body!!!
I had no choice but to run!!!!!
Run, Keiichi!!!!!!!
changeReason: Japanese '逃げろ' means 'Run!/Flee!'; new translation 'Run' is more direct and accurate than original's 'Get out of here.'

TextENGNew: Intellect, reason -- anything that required the luxury of thought was spilling from my head, one thing after another...
Even as branches scratched up my arms and cheeks, I felt no pain.
Even my heart, pounding mechanically, registered neither pain nor fatigue.
Every cell in my body simply wanted one thing -- to live...
It desired nothing else.
......So it had no complaints to voice.
And so, of course, I wouldn't feel fatigue...
I just...... ran.
I ran recklessly onward in whatever direction felt like forward.
...Even if no one had been chasing me, I wouldn't have stopped running...
Where I was headed -- I no longer knew or cared.
If I turned around, I felt a presence right behind me.
That presence was clamped onto me like a shadow, driving me forward without pause.
One misstep...... and I'd be devoured.
That's what I believed.
So I didn't look back.
I didn't stop.
I ran and ran.@k With everything I had...!!!
Kana kana kana kana......
changeReason: Japanese 'カナカナ' is the actual onomatopoeia for higurashi cicadas; new 'Kana kana' preserves the Japanese sound while original's 'Kwakwakwakwa' is an inaccurate rendering.

TextENGNew: It was the evening cry of the higurashi.
...The voices of those who had tried to tell me something, and in the end, never could --@k their weeping.
changeReason: Old says 'I finally heard it' (the narrator succeeded in hearing); JPN ついにかなわなかった means the victims 'ultimately could never succeed' in telling him — opposite outcome.

TextENGNew: Would I... join them?
Kana kana kana kana kana......
Only the higurashi knew.
......They knew everything.
They surely knew.
So... I ran toward wherever I could hear more of their cries.
But the cries...... retreated with every step I ran.@k ...I couldn't reach them.
Why are you running away from me, everyone...!!!
Was it my fault?
Was I the one to blame?!
Then I'll apologize!!
I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry......
I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry......
I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry.@k I'm sorry......
I felt that only the higurashi knew.
